textPlain: I was hoping for a Richie Hawtin[1] retrospective!1: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumed_(Plastikman_album)

I find the audio-visual experience (tilted elements, slightly blurry or shaky, semi-kinetic and burpy cronchy weird noises) of this website jarring as the act of over-consumption itself. Nicely done.A perfect metaphor.

I love this.Maybe these are incompatible desires, but I would really like some kind of system that allows me to own and present my own music listening data while also allowing me to interoperate with a broader music listening culture.Newsletters and blogs are great for discovery, but I also really value the way my last.fm has allowed me to recall a band I used to listen to a decade ago because I can remember a few of their contemporaries that are neighbours in the Similar Artists graph.
Very cool - how is the data being integrated under the hood?
How do you track all of these data points per day?
That is work, the food seems to be unique
Should have turned down the volume on my phone.
Wow, this diet looks very healthy, but on some days the calorie intake seems very low.
That can't be all the food their eating right? That's like 1/5th of the calories I eat as an athletic man
this is an amazing set of websites, coherently designed
Websites really need a warning before they play an incredibly loud sound without any notice. Infuriating.
I would also love to know how OP integrated logging these! I'm interested in this kind of tracking but I haven't found an ergonomic way to use it (not that I've tried hard).

I wonder if the phone has an auto volume off feature after no sound plays after n minutes?  I have an app on my mac called AutoMute that does similar, but it just mutes my mac whenever my headphones get disconnected.
Some of the days are pretty wild.September 15: Two plates of popcorn + pretzels.September 13: Three packs of gummy bears. Carrots and pretzels.
